title: Eduversal AI Competency Framework v1.0 — Appendices
sourceDocument: AI Competency Framework — For Eduversal Partner Schools (v1.0 PDF)

_meta.purpose: Verbatim canonical content for Appendices A-D of the Eduversal AI Competency Framework v1.0. Operational templates: teacher self-assessment, classroom observation guide, student literacy assessment examples, institutional self-assessment summary template.

appendixA_teacherSelfAssessment.title: Appendix A: Teacher Self-Assessment Tool
verbatim: Teachers should rate themselves against each domain and level using the following scale: Not Yet (not meeting this level), Developing (partially meeting this level), Achieved (consistently meeting this level).
For Domain A Knowledge and Understanding, assess against Foundation, Practitioner, and Leader level descriptors.
For Domain B Practical Application, assess against Foundation, Practitioner, and Leader level descriptors.
For Domain C Pedagogical Integration, assess against Foundation, Practitioner, and Leader level descriptors.
For Domain D Critical Evaluation, assess against Foundation, Practitioner, and Leader level descriptors.
For Domain E Professional Contribution, assess against Foundation, Practitioner, and Leader level descriptors.
Based on self-assessment, identify overall level (highest level where majority of domains are Achieved), key strengths, priority development areas, and professional learning goals.
appendixA_teacherSelfAssessment.phase2WidgetRef: Phase 2c implementation reads this appendix to render the self-assessment UI on TH /ai-self-assessment. Firestore writes to ai_competency_self_assessments/{uid}_{academicYear}.

appendixB_classroomObservationGuide.title: Appendix B: Classroom Observation Guide for AI Integration
verbatim: When observing lessons involving AI integration, observers should consider the following questions.
Regarding pedagogical purpose: Is AI use clearly connected to learning objectives? Does AI enhance rather than replace meaningful learning? Is there clear value added by AI integration?

Regarding quality of implementation: Has AI-generated content been appropriately reviewed and refined? Does the teacher exercise professional judgment in AI use? Is the implementation smooth and effective?
Regarding student engagement: Are students engaged in meaningful thinking and learning? Is AI supporting rather than replacing student effort? Are students developing skills and understanding?
Regarding differentiation: Is AI used to address diverse learner needs? Do all students benefit from AI integration? Are adjustments made for different student groups?
Regarding student AI literacy: Are students developing AI literacy through the lesson? Is responsible AI use modelled and taught? Do students demonstrate critical evaluation?
appendixB_classroomObservationGuide.usageNote: Subject Leaders, Academic Coordinators, and the Eduversal Academic Directorate use this guide during walkthroughs and appraisal visits. Observation outcomes feed into the Domain 4 (Teaching and Learning) institutional maturity rating.

appendixC_studentLiteracyAssessmentExamp.title: Appendix C: Student AI Literacy Assessment Examples

primary.verbatim: Primary Level Example: Ask students to explain what AI is in their own words and give two examples of AI they have encountered. Ask them to explain one thing AI can do well and one thing humans can do better than AI.

lowerSecondary.verbatim: Lower Secondary Example: Provide students with an AI-generated text on a topic they have studied. Ask them to identify any errors or questionable claims and explain how they would verify the accuracy of the information.

upperSecondary.verbatim: Upper Secondary Example: Present students with a scenario involving potential AI use for an academic task. Ask them to evaluate whether AI use would be appropriate, explain their reasoning with reference to academic integrity principles, and describe how they would use AI responsibly if appropriate.

appendixC_studentLiteracyAssessmentExamp.usageNote: These examples illustrate the assessment style expected at each age band. Teachers adapt to their subject context. Practical-layer classroom-activities.json builds on these patterns at activity level.

appendixD_institutionalSelfAssessmentTem.title: Appendix D: Institutional Self-Assessment Summary Template
verbatim: For each domain (Strategy and Leadership, Policy and Compliance, Staff Capability, Teaching and Learning, Student Outcomes, Infrastructure and Resources) record: Current Level (1-5), Key Evidence, Strengths, Areas for Improvement, and Target Level.
Overall school AI maturity level is calculated as the average across domains, rounded to nearest level.
Priority actions for coming year should be identified based on assessment results.

computedField.overallLevel: Average of currentLevel across 6 domains, rounded to nearest integer.

appendixD_institutionalSelfAssessmentTem.phase3WidgetRef: Phase 3a implementation reads this appendix to render the institutional maturity self-assessment UI on AH (school-side) and CH /ai-maturity-admin (appraisal queue). Firestore writes to ai_maturity_assessments/{schoolId}_{academicYear}.

documentInformation.effectiveDate: To be determined by Eduversal Academic Directorate
documentInformation.reviewCycle: Annual review, next review due one year from effective date
